nodes.title: Audit bdboard fidelity to the bd feature surface
nodes.description: Umbrella for a from-scratch coverage audit: does bdboard ACCURATELY REFLECT bd-based workflows? bdboard is a general-purpose, read-mostly FastAPI+HTMX dashboard that any developer can point at ANY bd workspace. The beads field guide (../training/beads/docs, 9 chapters) documents the full bd feature surface. For each of 9 capability areas we ask: (1) AVAILABLE - what the field guide says bd offers (chapter ref); (2) REFLECTED - what bdboard actually surfaces/displays (cite src file:line + notes/catalog/*.md); (3) GAPS - what bdboard misrepresents, hides, or cannot show, each with a P0-P4 severity and a one-line follow-up. Precedent: notes/analysis/bead-chain-coverage/ in the bead_chain plugin (README + _template.md + _scaffold.py + 9 section files + GAPS.md). FRAMING CONSTRAINT (load-bearing): bead-chain enforces one bead in_progress at a time; bdboard does NOT and SHOULD NOT. Multiple in_progress beads are normal for the projects bdboard serves. Do NOT record 'does not enforce single-WIP' as a gap. DO record any bead-chain-ism (serial-drive / single-WIP assumption) baked into bdboard's display as a fidelity bug. Work the leaves, not this epic.
nodes.acceptance:
Success Criteria:
- All 9 capability-area sections are filled (AVAILABLE / REFLECTED / GAPS) and marked done.
- Every gap carries a P0-P4 severity and a verbatim one-line follow-up.
- A synthesis (GAPS.md + filled capability matrix) consolidates the 9 sections and hands a planner a ranked, ready-to-pour follow-up shortlist.
- The audit explicitly treats multi-in_progress as normal and flags any single-WIP assumption in bdboard as a finding (not a feature).

nodes.title: Scaffold notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage (README, template, scaffold, 9 stubs)
nodes.description: Reproduce the bead-chain-coverage scaffold for bdboard so the 9 area audits stay DRY and traceable. Create notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/ with: (a) README.md - index + the 9-area matrix skeleton + the bdboard source map per area + the reframed severity rubric; (b) _template.md - the per-section shape: AVAILABLE / REFLECTED / GAPS(P0-P4) + severity rubric (underscore prefix = excluded from rendered index); (c) _scaffold.py - one-shot idempotent seeder writing the 9 NN-<area>.md stubs (mirror the precedent _scaffold.py); (d) the 9 seeded stubs themselves. Model every file on /Users/a0w0f56/.code_puppy/plugins/bead_chain/notes/analysis/bead-chain-coverage/. The REFLECTED axis (replacing the precedent's LEVERAGED) asks what bdboard DISPLAYS, citing src/bdboard/*.py + templates/partials/*.html + notes/catalog/*.md.
nodes.design:
Severity rubric for a DISPLAY tool (put in README + _template):
- P0: display misrepresents bead state so a user decides wrong (e.g. blocked shown as Ready; reversed dependency direction - cf. bdboard-fjk).
- P1: a board-meaning concept is silently wrong or absent (e.g. a status that has no lane; a gate bead shown as plain work).
- P2: capability unsurfaced where showing it would materially improve fidelity.
- P3: minor; narrow impact or workaround exists.
- P4: cosmetic / future-proofing.
Source map per area lives in README; each area bead's design field carries its starting-point pointers.
nodes.acceptance:
- notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/ exists with README.md, _template.md, _scaffold.py, and 9 NN-<area>.md stubs.
- README carries the 9-area matrix skeleton, per-area bdboard source map, and the reframed (display-tool) P0-P4 rubric.
- _template.md uses AVAILABLE / REFLECTED / GAPS sections (REFLECTED replaces the precedent's LEVERAGED).
- _scaffold.py is idempotent (skips stubs that already have findings) and regenerates all 9 stubs.
- Each stub names its owner audit bead id and its field-guide chapter.

nodes.title: Audit area 1: Anatomy of a bead - field fidelity
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/01-anatomy.md. AVAILABLE: the ~35 bead fields + 9+1 types per field-guide-01-anatomy-of-a-bead.html. REFLECTED: which fields/types bdboard renders in the bead detail modal, field rows, card, and raw view - cite src/bdboard/bd.py (field parsing) + templates/partials/bead_modal.html, field_row.html, bead_card.html + notes/catalog/bead-modal.md, bead-inline-edit.md, bead-raw.md. GAPS: every field bd exposes that bdboard drops, truncates, mislabels, or renders ambiguously; every bead type with no/incorrect visual treatment.
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/bd.py (bead JSON parse), templates/partials/field_row.html, bead_modal.html, bead_card.html, bead_priority_badge.html, notes/catalog/bead-modal.md + bead-raw.md + bead-inline-edit.md. Check: are all 10 types (incl. milestone, gate, molecule, decision, spike, story) visually distinguishable? Are acceptance/design/notes/labels/estimate/assignee/external_ref/spec_id all surfaced?
nodes.acceptance:
- 01-anatomy.md filled: AVAILABLE cites field-guide-01; REFLECTED cites bdboard file:line + catalog docs; GAPS each have P0-P4 + one-line follow-up.
- Coverage of fields bd exposes vs fields bdboard renders is explicit (which are dropped/mislabeled).
- Type-by-type visual treatment assessed (all 10 types).
- Section Status flipped to done.

nodes.title: Audit area 2: Dependency graph - edge fidelity & direction
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/02-dependency-graph.md. AVAILABLE: the 12 typed edges + 'depends on' direction semantics per field-guide-02-dependency-graph.html. REFLECTED: how bdboard shows dependencies/relationships and whether direction + edge-type labels are correct - cite the dep rendering in field_row.html / bead_modal.html and the known dep-edge-direction handling (bug bdboard-fjk, memory 'dep-edge-direction'). GAPS: reversed/ambiguous direction, raw dependency_type shown instead of a meaningful relationship label, blocks vs advisory edges conflated, no DAG/graph visualization (lanes only). This area is the highest P0 risk - a reversed edge makes a user act on the wrong prerequisite.
nodes.design: Start points: templates/partials/field_row.html (deps block), bead_modal.html, src/bdboard/bd.py, src/bdboard/derive/lanes.py (Blocked derivation). Cross-ref bug bdboard-fjk and the kennel 'dep-edge-direction' rule: bd reports 'blocks' on BOTH sides; the displayed label must depend on direction AND type. Check whether advisory edges (related/relates-to/tracks/discovered-from/caused-by/validates/supersedes/until) are surfaced and correctly NOT treated as blockers.
nodes.acceptance:
- 02-dependency-graph.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4 severities.
- Edge-direction correctness explicitly assessed (cite bdboard-fjk / dep-edge-direction rule); any reversal/ambiguity rated P0.
- blocks vs the 11 advisory edge types: which are surfaced, which are (correctly) non-blocking, which are mislabeled.
- Whether absence of a true DAG view is a gap (and at what severity) is recorded.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 3: Status lifecycle - 7 statuses vs the lanes
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/03-status-lifecycle.md. AVAILABLE: the 7 bd statuses + idiomatic transitions per field-guide-03-status-lifecycle.html. REFLECTED: bdboard derives 5 lanes (Deferred/Blocked/Ready/In-Progress/Closed) in src/bdboard/derive/lanes.py from bd list snapshots. GAPS: every bd status with no lane or a wrong-lane mapping (e.g. pinned/hooked/blocked-the-status vs blocked-the-derivation); whether transition affordances reflect bd's real lifecycle. CRITICAL for this area: confirm bdboard correctly shows MULTIPLE in_progress beads (general-purpose, NOT single-WIP). Flag any single-WIP assumption as a fidelity bug; do NOT flag multi-WIP itself as a gap.
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/derive/lanes.py (lane derivation), templates/partials/lanes.html, bead_card.html, notes/catalog/board-lanes.md, notes/design/bdboard-o9v.6/status-transition-affordance-design.md. Map all 7 bd statuses to lanes; identify statuses (pinned, hooked, and any operational-state axis) that fall through. Verify the In-Progress lane renders N>1 beads cleanly.
nodes.acceptance:
- 03-status-lifecycle.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- A full 7-status -> lane mapping table; every unmapped/mis-mapped status rated.
- Explicit confirmation that multiple simultaneous in_progress beads render correctly; any baked-in single-WIP assumption recorded as a finding.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 4: Memories & recall - the memory view
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/04-memories-recall.md. AVAILABLE: the 4 memory verbs + prime injection + recall ranking per field-guide-04-memories-and-recall.html. REFLECTED: bdboard's memory page (list/search/create/forget) - cite templates/memory.html + partials/memory_list.html + notes/catalog/memory-list.md, memory-create.md, memory-delete.md and the bd.py memory calls. GAPS: any recall/scoping/ranking semantics bd exposes that the UI hides or misrepresents (e.g. wing/scope, BM25 ranking, key collisions, upsert-vs-append semantics).
nodes.design: Start points: templates/memory.html, partials/memory_list.html, partials/memory_skeleton.html, src/bdboard/bd.py (memory verbs), notes/catalog/memory-*.md, notes/design/bdboard-5p1/memory-view-design.md. Check whether scope/wing, ranking order, and upsert semantics are faithfully conveyed.
nodes.acceptance:
- 04-memories-recall.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- The 4 verbs + recall semantics mapped to UI affordances; misrepresentations rated.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 5: Formulas & molecules - pour UI & mol display
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/05-formulas-molecules.md. AVAILABLE: formulas, mol verbs, mol-type (work/patrol/swarm/wisp), cook per field-guide-05-formulas-and-molecules.html. REFLECTED: bdboard's formula picker + pour form + result, and how molecule/epic container beads are displayed - cite partials/formula_form.html, formula_list.html, formula_pour_result.html + notes/catalog/pour-formula.md + app.py pour route + the grouping-node display decision (notes/design/bdboard-ain.2). GAPS: mol-type blindness (is a patrol/swarm/wisp visually distinct from plain work?), whether poured molecule structure (parent/children rollup) is legible, whether the pour write-surface misrepresents anything.
nodes.design: Start points: templates/partials/formula_form.html, formula_list.html, formula_pour_result.html, src/bdboard/app.py (pour endpoints), src/bdboard/bd.py (mol/formula calls), notes/catalog/pour-formula.md, notes/decisions/0007-formula-pour-ui-write-surface.md, notes/design/bdboard-ain.2/grouping-node-display-decision.md.
nodes.acceptance:
- 05-formulas-molecules.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- mol-type (work/patrol/swarm/wisp) display distinctness assessed; molecule rollup legibility assessed.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 6: Gates & coordination - are gates visible?
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/06-gates-coordination.md. AVAILABLE: gate types (human/timer/gh:run/gh:pr/bead), merge-slots, await-id, gate check/resolve per field-guide-06-gates-and-coordination.html. REFLECTED: how (if at all) bdboard surfaces gate beads, gate state, and merge-slot coordination - cite bd.py / app.py / lanes.py and the bead modal. GAPS: if gate beads are shown as plain work (no gate-state affordance, no await condition) that is a board-meaning P1; whether a pending/resolved gate is distinguishable; whether timer/gh gates show their condition.
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/derive/lanes.py, src/bdboard/bd.py, templates/partials/bead_modal.html, field_row.html. Likely finding: gates are not first-class in bdboard. Determine whether a gate bead is even fetched and how it lanes; rate the fidelity impact for a coordinating user.
nodes.acceptance:
- 06-gates-coordination.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- Whether gate beads are distinguishable from plain work, and whether gate state/await-id is surfaced, is explicitly rated.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 7: Swarms - execution hints, skills, parallel work
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/07-swarms.md. AVAILABLE: swarm mol-type, waves, skills, execution_* metadata hints, parallel groups, audit log per field-guide-07-swarms.html. REFLECTED: bdboard's activity swim lane + bead audit panel + any display of skills/execution_*/parallel-group - cite src/bdboard/derive/history.py, templates/partials/bead_audit.html + notes/catalog/bead-audit.md. GAPS: are skills/execution_* hints / parallel_group surfaced at all? Does the activity/audit view convey concurrent multi-agent work? CRITICAL: because bdboard is general-purpose, parallel/multi-in_progress swarm work is a first-class scenario it must render faithfully - explicitly assess N concurrent in_progress beads and flag any serial-only assumption.
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/derive/history.py (activity derivation), templates/partials/bead_audit.html, notes/catalog/bead-audit.md, src/bdboard/bd.py (metadata/skills parse). bd exposes execution_* under metadata and skills as a field; check whether bdboard reads/shows either. Tie to area 3's multi-WIP confirmation.
nodes.acceptance:
- 07-swarms.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- skills + execution_* + parallel_group display coverage assessed; concurrent multi-agent legibility assessed.
- Any serial-only / single-WIP assumption recorded as a finding (multi-WIP is NOT a gap).
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 8: Data layer (Dolt) - freshness & sync visibility
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/08-data-layer.md. AVAILABLE: Dolt-backed storage, dolt push/pull/remote, refs/dolt/data sync, issues.jsonl deprecation per field-guide-08-data-layer.html. REFLECTED: bdboard's runtime source-of-truth (bd CLI --json, never issues.jsonl), the watchfiles noms/ watcher, SSE live refresh, and the store cache - cite src/bdboard/watcher.py, store.py, events.py + notes/decisions/0003 & 0004 & 0005 + notes/catalog/store-cache.md, sse-live-refresh.md. GAPS: does bdboard convey sync state at all (is the board stale vs origin? unpushed local writes?); does it surface that it is read-only and never writes/exports; any freshness edge case (e.g. window-bounded closed lane hiding old closures).
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/watcher.py (noms watching, non-recursive), src/bdboard/store.py (three-way split cache), src/bdboard/events.py (SSE), notes/decisions/0003-beads-sync-via-dolt-git-refs.md, 0004-runtime-source-of-truth-bd-cli-json.md, 0005-live-refresh-architecture.md, notes/catalog/store-cache.md + sse-live-refresh.md. Freshness is bdboard's core promise - assess whether the board can silently show stale or window-clipped data without signaling it.
nodes.acceptance:
- 08-data-layer.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- Freshness + sync-state visibility assessed; any silent-staleness path rated.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Audit area 9: Quality & hygiene - lint/cycles/orphans surfacing
nodes.description: Fill notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/09-quality-hygiene.md. AVAILABLE: bd lint, dep cycles, orphans, stale, preflight per field-guide-09-quality-and-hygiene.html. REFLECTED: whether bdboard surfaces any graph-hygiene signal (lint warnings on a bead, a dependency cycle, an orphan, a stale bead) - cite bd.py / app.py / lanes.py / templates. GAPS: if a bead failing bd lint (missing required sections) looks identical to a healthy one, or a cycle/orphan is invisible, that hides correctness problems from the very people best placed to fix them.
nodes.design: Start points: src/bdboard/bd.py, src/bdboard/app.py, src/bdboard/derive/lanes.py, templates/partials/bead_modal.html + field_row.html. Likely finding: bdboard shows no hygiene signals (it is a viewer, not a linter). Rate whether surfacing lint/cycle/orphan/stale is a fidelity improvement and at what severity; note this is enrichment, not a viewer-SRP violation.
nodes.acceptance:
- 09-quality-hygiene.md filled with AVAILABLE/REFLECTED/GAPS + P0-P4.
- Whether any hygiene signal (lint/cycles/orphans/stale) reaches the UI is explicitly assessed.
- Section Status done.

nodes.title: Synthesize bdboard coverage GAPS.md + capability matrix
nodes.description: Mechanically merge the 9 completed section findings into notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/GAPS.md and fill the capability matrix in README.md - mirroring notes/analysis/bead-chain-coverage/GAPS.md. No new analysis: consolidate, dedup across sections (e.g. a single root cause surfacing in multiple areas), rank by severity-then-value, and emit a ranked, ready-to-pour FB-N follow-up shortlist with suggested bd create commands. Include an executive summary, a 'single biggest insight', and a 'what is explicitly NOT a gap' section that records multi-in_progress / general-purpose use as the intended design (so a future planner does not re-litigate it). This bead files NO beads itself - it hands a planner the shortlist.
nodes.design: Mirror GAPS.md structure: Executive summary -> capability matrix (A?/Reflected?/top sev/gap count/headline) -> severity x value ranking (P0..P4 deduped inventory with Source cites) -> dedup/cross-section reconciliation notes -> prioritized FB-N shortlist with bd create snippets -> 'what is NOT a gap'. Treat multi-WIP / general-purpose as a deliberate boundary, not a defect.
nodes.acceptance:
- GAPS.md exists: executive summary, deduped severity x value inventory (every gap traceable to a section row), and an FB-N ready-to-pour shortlist with bd create snippets.
- README capability matrix filled (A? / Reflected? / top sev / gap count / headline per area).
- A 'what is explicitly NOT a gap' section records multi-in_progress / general-purpose use as intended design.
- Files no beads itself.
